title: Humor
questions: In 'lonely hearts' columns people trying to find a partner often require that he or she should have a GSOH (a good sense of humour). How important do you think it is to have a good sense of humour in relationships?
Do you have a good sense of humour? Does your partner? And your teacher/boss...?
Do you know anyone who has an infectious laugh? How often do you laugh?
Are you good at telling jokes? Do you know any good (clean!) jokes?
Do you enjoy playing practical jokes on people? Have you ever been the victim of a practical joke?
Small children often say funny things. If you have children, what are the funniest things your children have said to you?
Do you enjoy watching humorous programmes such as sitcoms (situation comedies), funny films, cartoons, etc. Why/not?
Who is your favourite comedy actor/stand up comic? Why do you find him/her so funny?
Who is the funniest person you know? In what way is this person funny?
People of different nationalities seem to find different things funny. Why do you think there are national variations in humour?
British comedians have a tradition of making fun of the 'mother-in-law'. Do you have the same tradition in your country? Who/What do comedians in your country make fun of in particular?
Why do humans have a sense of humour? What purpose does it serve?
